title: Paris coup d'état and massacres of 1418
highlights:
On 12–13 June, a mob attacked the prisons where the Armagnacs and their supporters were held. Between one and two thousand people were killed. Among the dead were a constable of France, a chancellor of France, four bishops and several university professors.
